rulings.Case Title: GALINA URMAN VS. LEAD CLICK MEDIA, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 19, 2009, line 2, DEFENDANT FIRST ADVANTAGE CORPORATION, LEADCLICK MEDIA, INC. (LEADCLICK) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for Summary Judgment/Summary Adjudication is denied. Regarding the first cause of action, a triable issue of fact is raised whether defendant's conduct was sufficiently severe or pervasive to alter the conditions of plaintiff's employment and create an abusive work environment. Given the short period of time that the alleged harassment occurred, late July or August to September 2007, the conduct could be viewed as pervasive. (Plaintiff's depo 82-105.) A triable issue of fact is raised as to the second cause of action whether defendants breached their duty to investigate and prevent harassment. Plaintiff's evidence indicated that after defendants took action, harassment continued in that Boyd and Lombrozo "picked on" plaintiff. (Plaintiff's depo 150:19-151:6; 333:2-335:25; 361:3 - 362:25) Plaintiff raises a triable issue of fact as to the third cause of action for retaliation in that she complained to HR and that Boyd and Lombrozo's alleged abuse increased after her complaints. Both Boyd and Lombrozo criticized plaintiff for going to HR and complaining to Bennett. (Plaintiff's depo pg. 208.) This evidence shows a retaliatory animus. A triable issue of fact is therefore raised regarding constructive discharge supporting the wrongful termination in violation of public policy fourth cause of action. Defendant fails to shift the burden of proof as to plaintiff's request for punitive damages. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: 1054 KEARNY,LLC,DBA HEAVEN MINI-THEATER VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 19, 2009, line 3, CROSS COMPLAINANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, Motion For Preliminary Injunction. Motion for Preliminary Injunction is granted. When a government entity seeks to enjoin violations of local statutes that specifically provide for injunctive relief as a means of enforcement, the government need only establish a probability of prevailing on the merits. ITT Corp. vs. County of Imperial (1983) 35 Cal. 3d 63. A rebuttable presumption arises that the balance of harm favors the public interest over the private interest, and the burden shifts to cross-defendant to prove irreparable injury. The City sues pursuant to S.F. Planning Code sections 176 and 790.36, and is authorized to seek injunctive relief for any violation of the Planning Code. The City estabished that it is reasonably likely to prevail on the merits. The cease and desist orders and notice of violation establish that the club was operating without a permit. The Delaney declaration supports a citation for prostitution at the club, Gaines saw females dancing on an elevated pole, and a flyer advertized "Totally Nude Dances." Cross-defendant fails to show irerparable harm if an injunction is granted. Cross-defendant's claim of economic harm is compensable if they prevail at trial and the property may be used in a manner that does not violate the injunction. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GABRIELA V. MEJIA VS. KAISER PERMANENTE, A BUSINESS ORGANIZATION UNKNOWN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 19, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT PATRICIA GILES AMENDED DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Off Calendar, as moot. Defendant dismissed on August 7, 2009. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DARREN MATTE et al VS. JOHN KELLEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 19, 2009, line 7, PLAINTIFF DARREN MATTE, JOHN CHRISTIANSEN, and, MICHELLE WOHL Motion To Compel Arbitration. Motion to compel arbitration is granted. Arbitration clause binds John Kelley. (See reasoning in Keller Const. Co., Inc. vs. Kashani (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d 222.) Arbitration clause binds Kelley's Tavern LLC which adopted and ratified the preincorporation agreements executed on its behalf. The Loose claim does not exempt this controversy from arbitration. Plaintiffs have not waived their right to arbitrate. =(302/CWW)
